assumptions: the first release should focus on one hero product only
customers prefer a fast guided buying flow over a complex onboarding flow
scan instructions must be visual and short
risks: too many steps before checkout may reduce conversion
unclear scan guidance may cause failed scans
too much technical language may reduce trust
next_required_validation: Hermes review before app UX implementation
primary_user_goal: Understand the ZILFIT product quickly, complete the scan flow with confidence, and purchase with minimal friction.
friction_points: too much explanation before the user starts
asking for account creation before payment
showing multiple product options too early
ux_recommendation: Keep the first experience focused on one hero product, one guided scan path, and one fast checkout path. Use short visual instructions, strong trust cues, and mobile-first CTA placement.
next_design_step: Create a screen-by-screen structure with CTA text, scan prompts, and trust-building microcopy for the first ZILFIT mobile flow.
